# llms.txt — Navigate Health Insurance Services · Resources Hub
# Rules of the House for AI agents building or editing pages on this site.
# Last updated: 2026-05-06

## Site overview

This is the Medicare Resources Hub for Navigate Health Insurance Services
(DBA of CWC Medicare LLC), an independent health insurance agency.
Owner/operator: Chris Cockey, licensed health insurance agent.
Domain: learn.navigatehealthinsurance.com
Host: Netlify (free tier)
Main marketing site (separate): navigatehealthinsurance.com (Cloudflare Pages)

## 1. Branding — use the design system, nothing else

All styling MUST use the CSS custom properties defined in `/styles.css`.
Never hard-code colors, font families, font sizes, spacing values, or
border-radius values. If you need a token that doesn't exist, propose it
as an addition to :root — do not inline a one-off value.

Key variables:
  --brand-primary     #1B3A52  (navy)
  --brand-accent      #E74C4C  (red — CTAs only)
  --brand-blue        #5FB8E8  (links, info callouts)
  --brand-cream       #FAF6F1  (page background)
  --text-main         #1E2A35  (body copy)
  --text-secondary    #1B3A52  (secondary copy — navy, NEVER gray)
  --font-heading      Fraunces
  --font-body         Inter

Senior accessibility rule: NEVER use gray text. All text must be black,
near-black (#1E2A35), or navy (#1B3A52). This is a medical-audience
accessibility requirement, not a style preference.

## 2. HIPAA — no native Netlify Forms

Do NOT use Netlify's built-in form handling (the `netlify` attribute,
`data-netlify="true"`, Netlify Functions that process form submissions,
etc.). Submitted data would be stored on Netlify infrastructure, which
is not configured for HIPAA compliance in this deployment.

For any lead capture, contact form, RSVP, or data-collection need:
  → Use a Google Forms iframe embed.
  → The component class is `.form-embed` (defined in styles.css).
  → See `spoke-template.html` for the reference implementation.

If a page does not need lead capture, omit the form section entirely.

## 3. Compliance — TPMO disclaimer on every page

Every page deployed to this site MUST include the TPMO (Third-Party
Marketing Organization) disclaimer in the footer. The canonical text
lives in the `<div class="tpmo-disclaimer">` block in `index.html`.

Copy it exactly. Do not paraphrase, abbreviate, or restyle it.
The disclaimer must be visible (not hidden behind a toggle or accordion).

CMS requires the generic website TPMO disclaimer to be used VERBATIM
on pages where the visitor's location is unknown. Do NOT add state
lists, carrier counts, or any other modifications to paragraph one.

Exact disclaimer text (two paragraphs):

  "We do not offer every plan available in your area. Any information
  we provide is limited to those plans we do offer in your area.
  Please contact Medicare.gov or 1-800-MEDICARE (1-800-633-4227) to
  get information on all of your options."

  "Not connected with or endorsed by the United States government or
  the federal Medicare program. This is a solicitation of insurance.
  A licensed agent may contact you. Navigate Health Insurance Services
  is a DBA of CWC Medicare LLC."

## 4. Structure — subfolder convention

New resource pages (spokes) must live in their own subfolder with an
`index.html` file. This gives clean URLs on Netlify:

  /legislative-timeline/index.html  →  /legislative-timeline/
  /getting-started/index.html       →  /getting-started/
  /cost-estimator/index.html        →  /cost-estimator/

Start every new spoke from `spoke-template.html`. That template already
includes: the shared stylesheet link, header, Google Form placeholder,
footer with TPMO disclaimer, and mobile-optimized viewport meta.

Static assets (images, icons, PDFs) go in `/assets/`.

## 5. CTA and copy rules

- Primary CTA: "Schedule an Appointment" → Calendly link
  (https://calendly.com/navigate-insurance)
- Never put the word "free" in a button label. "Free" is fine in body
  copy — just not on the button itself.
- Never use the word "consultation." Use "appointment" or "meeting."
- Refer to Chris as "licensed health insurance agent" (short form) in
  most contexts. Use "licensed, independent health insurance agent"
  (long form) only in bio/about sections.
- Voice: first-person ("I'm Chris…"), low-fluff, patient, analytical.
  No hype, no superlatives applied to plans, no scare tactics.
- Every piece of content must include a clear CTA. Educational content
  without a next step works against the business.

## 6. Contact information

  Name:  Chris Cockey
  Phone: (303) 997-2505
  Email: chris@navigatehealthinsurance.com
  Calendly: https://calendly.com/navigate-insurance
  Business: Navigate Health Insurance Services (DBA of CWC Medicare LLC)
  States: CO, CA, NV, KS

Do NOT use cwcmedicare@gmail.com on any client-facing page.

## 7. Technical notes

- Google Fonts CDN is used (Fraunces + Inter). No self-hosted font files.
- The site is static HTML/CSS. No build step, no framework.
- Netlify handles SSL automatically for the custom domain.
- The root `styles.css` is shared across all pages via absolute path
  (`/styles.css`), so spokes in subfolders still reference it correctly.
